transcript:
SOURCE: Fathom (via HubSpot internal notes) — https://fathom.video/share/LxoHe4SmNVz8agHfVUU3LFK9Y1EPNws1

AI Meeting Summary ✨

Generated by Fathom // View Recording (68 mins - No highlights)

Deal Information

Prospect

Scott Levine, solo family law attorney based in Fort Lauderdale, FL (30+ years practicing). Currently a true solo with no staff; subleases office space (~$1,800/mo). Considering expansion/second branded office in Daytona/Space Coast area with thematic branding (NASCAR/SpaceX).

Call Context

Inbound referral from SMB Team client George Raris; Scott watched SMB Team’s “How 86 firms doubled revenue” video. Goal: increase volume and quality of leads beyond referrals; build a dependable pipeline, avoid low-fee, high-drama cases.

Seats

Solo now; anticipates hiring 1 full-time paralegal after adding ~5 new matters/month. Potential future hires (intake, collections) as pipeline grows.

Budget

Current marketing spend: ~$1,500–$2,000/mo all-in with existing vendor (includes site + PPC). Of that, only ~$800–$1,000/mo goes to Google ad spend; remainder to vendor fees.

Acknowledges SMB Team “is not cheap” but is open if ROI is clear.

Current State

Website handled by Transformational Outsourcing; PPC run by owner’s son.

Phones routed through office receptionist; no 24/7 coverage; some calls to Scott’s cell if not in office; likely missed/abandoned calls; no consistent reporting reviewed.

Monthly revenue: bad month ~$21–22k; good month ~$30k; occasional $40–45k when in trial (unsustainably intense).

Practice mix: family law (divorce, paternity, custody). Willing to do virtual or in-person.

Pain Points

Low-quality, low-retainer leads wasting time and risking bad reviews.

Inconsistent pipeline reliant on referrals; current PPC “sucks.”

Solo capacity bottleneck; hesitation to hire without predictable revenue.

Website/SEO misalignment hurting Maps and organic visibility (H1/title mismatch, keyword cannibalization).

Missed calls/out-of-hours gaps likely suppressing conversions.

Specific Requirements

Higher-quality lead flow willing/able to pay ($5k+ initial retainers; average case ~$15k; avoids $1.5k “messy” cases).

Guidance to scale intelligently: intake, staffing (A-player paralegal), pricing/retainer strategy, and operational coaching.

Flexibility to improve current site and PPC first; not forced to rebuild day one. Willing to cede website later if ROI warrants.

Support for opening a second, separately branded office (own door/suite for GMB) with local SEO for Daytona/Space Coast when timing is right.

Tech Stack

Website: likely WordPress (uncertain); Transformational Outsourcing maintains.

Phones: office-provided system; receptionist answers; unknown reporting access (can request).

PPC: Google Ads via vendor’s son.

No current answering service; open to Smith.ai (AI or human).

Objections

Cost sensitivity: expects SMB Team won’t be cheap; needs confidence in ROI. Status: Addressed with quick-win strategies (hours, intake, Maps), layered approach (ads now, SEO compounding, coaching).

Bandwidth/change management: wants handholding; not jumping “feet first.” Status: Addressed—team-based support (coach + marketing manager), staged rollout.

Concern about website control: doesn’t want forced takeover. Status: Addressed—can start without immediate site takeover; recommended later for performance.

Other Solutions Under Evaluation

Current vendor (Transformational Outsourcing + PPC by son). No other alternatives named.

Timeline

Near-term: improve current Fort Lauderdale pipeline first; target +5 matters/month to justify paralegal.

Medium-term: once cash flow stabilizes, launch Daytona/Space Coast brand with dedicated office for GMB.

Follow-up meeting set for Friday 11:30 AM Eastern.

Decision Maker

Scott Levine is primary. His wife is actively involved in operations/intake decisions and interested in the plan.

Procurement Process

Scott evaluates ROI and operational feasibility; likely to phase spend (shift budget from current vendor to SMB Team). Prefers proof via early wins (intake, Maps, PPC performance) before deeper commitments (site takeover, new office launch).

Next steps

Dan to:

Send follow-up materials, examples (incl. George, family law case studies), and recommendations.

Provide Smith.ai options (AI-first ~$95/mo; human from ~$300/mo) and intake booking-link workflow.

Share auditing insights (Maps hours strategy, H1/title corrections, directory hours alignment).

Scott to:

Expand business hours (already set to 8:30–8:30); arrange live answer through office to match hours; consider weekend coverage and/or Smith.ai.

Check phone system reporting for missed/abandoned calls.

Consider adjusting website H1s/titles and reducing keyword cannibalization; align directory hours before changing GMB to 24/7 if desired.

Schedule: Confirmed follow-up Friday 11:30 AM Eastern with Dan.

Q&A

Questions We Asked

What initially piqued your interest in SMB Team?

Referral from client George Raris; he was highly complimentary.

Primary goals: growth, time freedom, profit?

All of the above.

Practice focus: divorce vs. broader family law?

Don’t limit to “divorce” only; demographics skew divorce to 40+, but paternity and other family matters are rising. Divorce remains a critical keyword.

Who manages your website and PPC? How’s it performing?

Transformational Outsourcing runs site; PPC by owner’s son. PPC underperforming; website not a priority for Scott but open to guidance.

Current spend split (fees vs. ad spend)?

~$1.5–2k/mo total; only ~$800–1,000 to Google; remainder to vendor.

Monthly revenue range?

~$21–22k low; ~$30k typical; occasional $40–45k during trials.

Do you have support staff?

No; true solo.

How many additional clients/mo to justify a paralegal?

~5 more per month.

Retainers and rates?

Moving to $5k initial on most; can accept $3.5k with higher hourly. Hourly $400–$500.

Average case value and duration?

Avg ~$15k per case over time; rare $85–95k outliers.

Willing to expand hours and implement live answering?

Yes; immediately updated GMB to 8:30–8:30; will set answering coverage; open to weekend by appointment and 24/7 via service.

Questions They Asked

Can SMB Team work without taking over the website initially?

Yes. We can start with PPC/Maps/intake improvements and later take over site for bigger SEO gains.

Recommendations for 24/7 answering and AI intake?

Smith.ai recommended: AI-first plan starts ~$95/mo (covers ~50 calls/month); human plans from ~$300/mo. Provide booking-link workflow so reception/answering can schedule 10-min intakes.

How to handle hours expansion without conflicting listings?

Align hours messaging across website (homepage/footer/contact), directories (Apple/Bing/YP/Lawyers.com), then update Google Business Profile last to avoid conflicts and improve approval/visibility.

Feasibility of launching a second, separately branded office?

Yes. Secure a small dedicated suite with your name on the door (not generic coworking) to get GMB verified; build separate brand (DBA or new entity) localized to Daytona/Space Coast; we’ll handle Maps/SEO to stand it up cost-effectively once Fort Lauderdale is producing consistently.

Action Items

Set up 10-min intake booking link for receptionist/answering service - WATCH (5 secs)
Schedule office answering service to cover extended hours (incl weekends) - WATCH (5 secs)
Check phone system logs for missed/abandoned calls; share w/ Dan - WATCH (5 secs)
Update website footer/Contact to 24/7 by phone; then update directories/GBP to 24/7 - WATCH (5 secs)
Send Scott calendar invite for Fri 11:30 ET; then attend - WATCH (5 secs)
Email/text Scott case studies, examples, and contact info - WATCH (5 secs)
